context: You can talk about your day - now talk about where you spend it. Describing your home is one of the first real conversations you will have: where you live, what is in it, and who you live with.
explanation: Start with the place itself. casa is house or home, apartamento is an apartment, and habitación is a room inside it - "vivo en un apartamento" means I live in an apartment. Once you can name the place, you only need two small tools to describe it: hay (there is / there are) for what exists, and está (is located) for where things are. Those come next.

examples.note: hay = there is / there are - it never changes.
habitación = room; es describes what it is like.
está tells you where something is located.
con mi familia = with my family; vivo solo = I live alone.
